params.text:
What is Basic Life Support?

Basic Life Support (BLS) is the set of simple, life-saving actions anyone can take to keep a person alive when their heart or breathing has stopped, until emergency help arrives. It does not need any special equipment beyond a defibrillator where one is available.

When a person's heart stops — a cardiac arrest — blood stops flowing to the brain and vital organs. Every minute without chest compressions and defibrillation reduces the chance of survival. Acting quickly and confidently makes a real difference to whether that person lives.

In a care setting you may be first on the scene when a person you support collapses. Knowing what to do, and doing it straight away, is one of the most valuable skills you can have.

Important: this is awareness training

This course raises your awareness and helps you understand the principles of BLS and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR). It is based on current Resuscitation Council UK guidelines and NHS guidance.

It does not replace a hands-on, certificated BLS/CPR course. You cannot become competent in chest compressions or rescue breaths by reading alone. You must attend accredited, practical training where you practise on a manikin under a qualified instructor and are formally assessed. Please treat this module as preparation for, or a refresher alongside, that face-to-face training.
